summary: Created the US-844 bullet scoring QA design document at docs/test-design/us-844-bullet-scoring.md. Spexus current_context was read before the task and epic hierarchy. The design aligns to the current Score/scoring/settlement/render surfaces, defines numeric executable scenarios for contract success, contract failure, misere success/failure, progressive all-pass, deal-settlement transitions, next-deal continuation, and bullet completion, and separately marks the whist/final-settlement scenarios that remain blocked by the open scoring-rule gaps explicitly called out in PERSONAL-STD-063. The document also defines deterministic fixture baselines, score-delta expectations, UI assertions, accessibility/visual applicability, and follow-up mapping for MZ-402 through MZ-406. Minimal docs-only verification confirmed the document exists, is readable, and the final report JSON is valid.

tests_run.evidence: Readable final task report check succeeded.
Final task report JSON parsed successfully.
Header, current-source alignment, and fixture/scenario opening sections rendered correctly from the written document.

downstream_notes: Current src/domain/scoring.ts and src/domain/engine.ts cover contract success/failure, misere success/failure, progressive all-pass, and bullet-target completion, but do not yet update mutual-whist values or compute a final bullet settlement object.

Current src/ui/render.ts exposes settlementSummary and winnerSummary text only; later UI tasks need a real score table and final-result surface before AC-2426 and AC-2427 accessibility/visual checks can pass.
The provisional whist/final-settlement rows in docs/test-design/us-844-bullet-scoring.md are intentionally explicit TODO contracts so later workers do not silently invent incompatible rules.

PERSONAL-STD-063 still leaves exact whist/half-whist/undertrick formulas and the exact final bullet settlement formula unresolved; MZ-403, MZ-405, and MZ-406 must lock that scoring contract before US-844 can claim release-gate-complete runtime coverage.

verification_environment.notes: Per task instructions, npm run verify was not needed because the work stayed within QA design documentation and task-report artifacts only.

quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Relies on task execution order and recorded report metadata rather than an independent external audit.
Dependency reports informed the scoring-design scope and current-surface alignment only; this task did not rerun implementation verification.
This gate verifies documentation completeness and actionability, not runtime scoring behavior.
quality_gate_matrix.accepted_risk: Whist/final-settlement runtime behavior remains dependent on later implementation and scoring-contract lock-in.

quality_gate_matrix.limitations: The gap is documented, not resolved, in this task.
quality_gate_matrix.accepted_risk: Follow-up scoring tasks must not treat provisional whist/final-settlement assumptions as verified DOS-authentic formulas without an explicit product decision.

quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Accessibility and visual checks are specified but not executed in this docs-only task.
quality_gate_matrix.accepted_risk: UI score/result verification remains for MZ-405/MZ-406.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: PERSONAL-TASK-1153 scope; PERSONAL-EP-180 hierarchy; docs-only deliverable
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: No auth, backend, network, or sensitive data path exists in this task scope.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: test -r docs/test-design/us-844-bullet-scoring.md; test -r task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1153/attempt-1.json; jq empty task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1153/attempt-1.json; sed -n '1,80p' docs/test-design/us-844-bullet-scoring.md
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Verification was intentionally minimal because no production or test code changed.
This task contributes release-gate test design only and does not by itself satisfy the US-844 release gate.
quality_gate_matrix.accepted_risk: US-844 remains dependent on later implementation, scoring-contract lock, and UI verification tasks.
